2606.12234.title: On The Effectiveness-Fluency Trade-Off In LLM Conditioning: A Systematic Study
2606.12234.paper_summary: Controlling the output of Large Language Models (LLMs) is a central challenge for their reliable deployment, yet a clear understanding of the involved trade-offs remains elusive. Current approaches to conditioning are often evaluated with a narrow focus on their effectiveness at injecting or removing a target concept, neglecting generation quality. We systematically investigate a range of conditioning methods in both injection and removal scenarios. We find that efficient steering methods frequently achieve conditioning at a steep cost to fluency. Furthermore, we identify a critical yet previously overlooked interaction with the training paradigm: activation steering methods are far less effective on instruction-tuned models than on their base counterparts. Simple prompting and full-fledged supervised fine-tuning, on the other hand, are viable options for concept injection, but are not as good at concept removal. Finally, cheaply computed textual metrics highly correlate to costly LLM-as-judge scores, and provide insights on the behavior of conditioning methods.
2606.12234.paper_summary_zh: 控制大型語言模型（LLMs）的輸出是其可靠部署的核心挑戰，但對於所涉及的權衡仍然缺乏清晰的理解。<lb/>當前的條件化方法通常以狹隘的焦點評估其在注入或移除目標概念方面的有效性，忽視了生成質量。<lb/>我們系統性地調查了一系列條件化方法在注入和移除場景中的表現。<lb/>我們發現，高效的引導方法在流暢性上常常以高昂的代價來實現條件化。<lb/>此外，我們識別出一個關鍵但先前被忽視的與訓練範式的互動：激活引導方法在指令調整模型上的效果遠不如其基礎對應模型。<lb/>另一方面，簡單的提示和全面的監督微調是概念注入的可行選擇，但在概念移除方面表現不佳。<lb/>最後，廉價計算的文本指標與昂貴的LLM作為評判者的分數高度相關，並提供了對條件化方法行為的洞察。
2606.12234.author: Iuri Macocco et.al.
2606.12234.authors: Iuri Macocco, Pau Rodríguez, Arno Blaas, Luca Zappella, Marco Baroni, Xavier Suau
